summary: A superb MIT‑radio soundboard capture of a historically charged May 6, 1970 show, highlighted by an incendiary “Dancin’”, epic “Good Lovin'”/St. Stephen/NFA medley and a soaring “Morning Dew”.
review: The recording, taken from the band’s direct feed to MIT’s WTBS, is remarkably clear and loud, preserving every nuance of Jerry’s ferocious guitar and Phil’s melodic bass. Musically the performance is frequently called one of the year’s best – the jam‑laden “Dancin’”, a legendary “Good Lovin'” duet with Pigpen, a thunderous St. Stephen → NFA stretch and an emotionally charged “Morning Dew” all unfold against the tense backdrop of the Kent State protests.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance and the sound quality; the weighted average (70 % show, 30 % recording) lands in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Direct feed from the band’s amps yields crisp, loud, well‑balanced mix; occasional comments about slight roughness or choppiness but no serious drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows organically, with the high‑energy “Dancin’” opening the second half, a seamless St. Stephen → Not Fade Away/NFA sequence, and a closing arc that builds to an emotional “Morning Dew”. The political tension of the Kent State aftermath adds urgency without breaking momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for a ferocious, gritty, metallic‑twang guitar tone; his solos on “Dancin'”, “Good Lovin'” and the NFA jam are called ‘monster’, ‘ripping’ and ‘gritty’.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lauded for lead‑bass playing that drives improvisation; reviewers note his interplay with Jerry, pre‑show bass riff, and overall authority on the low end.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned mainly for vocal duels (e.g., with Pigpen on “Next Time You See Me”) and rhythmic support; no specific criticism.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as a ‘soundscape’, ‘great percussion’, and essential to the intensity of the jams.
